heading: Gifts to Federal employees
label: 7353 Gifts to Federal employees

L. 117–286, § 4(c)(10)136 Stat. 4354Oct. 13, 2008Dec. 27, 2022“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—GiftThe term ‘gift’ has the meaning given under , United States Code.section 13101(5) of title 5“(2).—Judicial officerThe term ‘judicial officer’ has the meaning given under , United States Code.section 13101(10) of title 5“(b).—Prohibition on Acceptance of Honorary Club MembershipsA judicial officer may not accept a gift of an honorary club membership with a value of more than $50 in any calendar year.”

L. 117–286, § 4(c)(10)136 Stat. 4354Oct. 13, 2008Dec. 27, 2022“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—GiftThe term ‘gift’ has the meaning given under , United States Code.section 13101(5) of title 5“(2).—Judicial officerThe term ‘judicial officer’ has the meaning given under , United States Code.section 13101(10) of title 5“(b).—Prohibition on Acceptance of Honorary Club MembershipsA judicial officer may not accept a gift of an honorary club membership with a value of more than $50 in any calendar year.”

children.text: seeking official action from, doing business with, or (in the case of executive branch officers and employees) conducting activities regulated by, the individual’s employing entity; or
whose interests may be substantially affected by the performance or nonperformance of the individual’s official duties.
Each supervising ethics office is authorized to issue rules or regulations implementing the provisions of this section and providing for such reasonable exceptions as may be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), a Member, officer, or employee may accept a gift pursuant to rules or regulations established by such individual’s supervising ethics office pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.label: B
children.text: No gift may be accepted pursuant to subparagraph (A) in return for being influenced in the performance of any official act.
Nothing in this section precludes a Member, officer, or employee from accepting gifts on behalf of the United States Government or any of its agencies in accordance with statutory authority.

Nothing in this section precludes an employee of a private sector organization, while assigned to an agency under chapter 37, from continuing to receive pay and benefits from such organization in accordance with such chapter.

children.label: c
children.text: A Member of Congress or an officer or employee who violates this section shall be subject to appropriate disciplinary and other remedial action in accordance with any applicable laws, Executive orders, and rules or regulations.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Standards of Official Conduct of the House of Representatives or the House of Representatives as a whole, for Members, officers, and employees of the House of Representatives;

children.label: B
children.text: the Select Committee on Ethics of the Senate, or the Senate as a whole, for Senators, officers, and employees of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Judicial Conference of the United States for judges and judicial branch officers and employees;

children.label: D
children.text: the Office of Government Ethics for all executive branch officers and employees; and

children.label: E
children.text: in the case of legislative branch officers and employees other than those specified in subparagraphs (A) and (B), the committee referred to in either such subparagraph to which reports filed by such officers and employees under subchapter I of chapter 131 of this title are transmitted under such subchapter, except that the authority of this section may be delegated by such committee with respect to such officers and employees; and
the term “officer or employee” means an individual holding an appointive or elective position in the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of Government, other than a Member of Congress.
